branch_points.description: DSAG adoption gap constraint activates — 83% unfamiliarity rate binds near-term BDC pipeline across all scenarios.

Critical gap identified: Reltio's technical capability alone does not resolve the DSAG trust deficit. The sales and change-management problem remains structurally independent of the product acquisition.
Scenario B branch point: No joint Reltio-Databricks integration roadmap exists at acquisition time. Scenario B is gated on a Sapphire 2026 announcement.
Scenario C blocking condition: Without a customer-facing simplification narrative and pricing reform, DSAG voices dominate and BDC pipeline growth is structurally blocked.

causal_chain: SAP acquires Reltio, adding AI entity resolution (golden record) capability to BDC
DSAG trust deficit (83% BDC unfamiliarity) constrains near-term pipeline regardless of Reltio's technical merit
Three strategic paths diverge: (A) Reltio as self-service onboarding accelerator, (B) Reltio extended via Databricks channel, (C) Reltio compounds migration complexity
Path A activation requires migration-phase pricing reform and low-friction MDM API exposure
Path B activation requires a joint SAP-Databricks announcement at Sapphire 2026
Path C is the default risk path if SAP does not proactively address migration friction within 6 months
Databricks alliance amplifies Path A and enables Path B if not constrained by open-format competing interests

reality_gap_analysis.announced_vs_realised_gap: SAP's acquisition announcement is product-centric (entity resolution capability) but does not address the customer-experience gap (mid-migration billing friction, DSAG trust deficit). The strategic logic is sound, but the go-to-market narrative lags the technical rationale.

signal_confidence_gaps.gap_description: 83% DSAG unfamiliarity is a lagging indicator — current awareness rate post-acquisition announcement is unknown
Databricks amplifier is assumed to activate for Reltio but no integration commitment has been announced

key_assumption_risks: Reltio's Spanner/GCP architecture compatibility with BDC integration layer is assumed but not confirmed
SAP's sales cycle can absorb a new MDM narrative while managing existing migration conversations
Databricks remains a committed amplifier and does not compete directly in the MDM space

applied_axioms.capability_transfer_axiom: When a target company's core capability directly addresses the acquirer's primary product weakness, integration speed determines competitive moat timing.
applied_axioms.trust_deficit_axiom: A platform with a documented trust deficit (quantified via third-party research) cannot fully benefit from a capability acquisition until the trust gap is independently addressed.
applied_axioms.ecosystem_amplifier_axiom: A pre-existing ecosystem alliance amplifies acquisition impact if the integration roadmap is jointly announced and credibly scoped.

rule_trace_references.application: Reltio entity resolution directly addresses BDC's core weakness (external data fragmentation). Integration speed is the moat-timing variable.
83% DSAG unfamiliarity is the trust deficit signal. Reltio acquisition cannot close this gap — it requires a separate pricing and migration narrative.
Databricks alliance amplifies Reltio impact only if a joint integration roadmap is publicly committed. Without Sapphire 2026 announcement, the amplifier remains latent.

outcome_evidence_links.outcome: Scenario A: BDC adoption accelerates via Reltio-enabled onboarding
supporting_evidence: Reltio entity resolution directly removes data fragmentation barrier (sig-sap-1)
SAP board exec quote confirms strategic intent: 'combining SAP and non-SAP data'

outcome_evidence_links.outcome: Scenario B: Reltio extends BDC to data-first enterprises via Databricks
supporting_evidence: Reltio's cloud-native Spanner architecture enables hyperscaler ecosystem compatibility
challenging_evidence: No joint Databricks-Reltio integration commitment at acquisition announcement
Databricks open-format strategy may conflict with SAP's One Domain Model

outcome_evidence_links.outcome: Scenario C: Reltio compounds migration complexity and adoption stalls
supporting_evidence: DSAG research: 83% BDC unfamiliarity with no announced improvement programme
Mid-migration billing friction: customers paying for unusable systems
Consultant quote: 'That line is going to haunt SAP's sales deck'
challenging_evidence: SAP's acquisition framing as simplification tool offers a counter-narrative
Reltio's existing enterprise customer base (Pfizer, Radisson, Warner Bros) provides credibility

counterfactual_conditions.description: What if SAP announced a migration-phase pricing reform simultaneously with the Reltio acquisition?
counterfactual_conditions.expected_impact: Would significantly reduce Scenario C risk and accelerate Scenario A timeline by addressing the trust deficit proactively.

counterfactual_conditions.description: What if Databricks announces competing MDM/entity resolution capability at Sapphire 2026?
counterfactual_conditions.expected_impact: Would neutralise sig-sap-4 amplifier and potentially convert Scenario B to a defensive positioning challenge.

narrative_summary: SAP's Reltio acquisition is strategically coherent — entity resolution directly addresses BDC's external data fragmentation problem. However, the acquisition logic does not automatically resolve the documented DSAG trust deficit or mid-migration billing friction that constrains near-term BDC pipeline. Scenario A (adoption acceleration) is the highest-confidence path and requires SAP to address pricing reform before Reltio capabilities reach GA. Scenario B (Databricks channel extension) offers incremental upside gated on a Sapphire 2026 joint announcement. Scenario C (adoption stall) is the dominant downside risk if SAP allows the current migration friction narrative to persist unchallenged.
